audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as the first whirlwind response: YHWH answers Job from storm, creation foundations, sea boundaries, dawn, depths, light/darkness, snow/hail, rain wilderness, stars, clouds, and wild creatures. Divine speech is treated as the book's climactic answer, not friend-theology.

verses.restored: Then the Eternal answered Job from the whirlwind and said:
Who is this darkening counsel by words without knowledge?
Gird your loins like a strong man; I will ask you, and you make it known to me.
Where were you when I founded the earth? Tell, if you know understanding.
Who set its measurements, if you know? Or who stretched a line upon it?
On what were its bases sunk, or who laid its cornerstone,
when the morning stars sang together and all the sons of the Divine shouted for joy?
Who shut in the sea with doors when it burst out from the womb,
when I made cloud its garment and thick darkness its swaddling band,
and prescribed my limit for it and set bars and doors,
and said, To here you may come, and no farther; here your proud waves shall stop?
Have you commanded the morning in your days, and made the dawn know its place,
to take hold of the edges of the earth, so the wicked are shaken out of it?
It is changed like clay under a seal, and things stand forth like a garment.
From the wicked their light is withheld, and the raised arm is broken.
Have you entered the springs of the sea, or walked in the recesses of the deep?
Have the gates of death been revealed to you, or have you seen the gates of deep shadow?
Have you understood the breadth of the earth? Tell, if you know all this.
Where is the way to the dwelling of light, and darkness, where is its place,
that you may take it to its boundary and discern the paths to its house?
You know, for you were born then, and the number of your days is great!
Have you entered the storehouses of snow, or seen the storehouses of hail,
which I have kept for the time of distress, for the day of battle and war?
By what way is light divided, or the east wind scattered over the earth?
Who has cut a channel for the flood, or a way for the thunderbolt,
to bring rain on a land with no human, on wilderness where no person is,
to satisfy waste and desolation, and make the tender grass sprout?
Does rain have a father? Or who has fathered drops of dew?
From whose womb did ice come out, and who gave birth to the frost of the heavens?
Waters hide themselves like stone, and the face of the deep is held fast.
Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades, or loosen the cords of Orion?
Can you bring out the constellations in their season, or guide the Bear with its children?
Do you know the statutes of the heavens? Can you set their rule on the earth?
Can you lift your voice to the clouds, so that a flood of waters covers you?
Can you send out lightning bolts, that they may go and say to you, Here we are?
Who has put wisdom in the inward parts, or given understanding to the mind?
Who can count the clouds by wisdom, and who can tip the jars of the heavens,
when dust hardens into a cast mass and clods cling together?
Can you hunt prey for the lion, or satisfy the life of young lions,
when they crouch in dens and lie in wait in the thicket?
Who provides for the raven its food, when its young cry to the Divine and wander without food?
